page: Amazon Web Services c3.large is a compute-optimized, third-generation virtual server instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor running at 2.8 GHz with an i386 CPU architecture. The instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, supported by a 25 MB L3 cache. It includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.88 GB of RAM per core, and comes bundled with 32 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud hardware. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as weak, while multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom ten percent. Memory bandwidth tests show poor throughput, with read-only large block transfers reaching 13.06 GB/sec and write operations at 11.39 GB/sec. Similarly, Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores reside in the bottom ten percent, reflecting its legacy hardware profile.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the c3.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation and local NVMe storage, but its older architecture limits its utility for modern, resource-intensive workloads. It is best suited for legacy applications, basic web serving, lightweight background tasks, or simple single-threaded operations that do not require high memory bandwidth or advanced vector extensions beyond basic AVX.
description: Amazon Web Services c3.large is a legacy compute-optimized instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor and an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor. Operating on an i386 architecture, it provides 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical core, 4.0 GB of system memory, and 32 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results indicate limited processing capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom decile of cloud servers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large block read speeds of 13.06 GB/sec. Given these performance characteristics and its older hardware profile, the c3.large is best reserved for basic web serving, lightweight background processes, and legacy applications that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high memory throughput or modern instruction sets.

tagline: A legacy compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe SSD storage for basic workloads.
